impact: `.b-btn`, `.b-nav-link`, `.b-tab-strip a`, `.b-link`, `.b-nav-dropdown-item`, `.b-card`, `.b-row`, mobile-menu links, and the notification bell all define `:hover` only — there are no `:focus-visible` or `:focus` rules anywhere in base.html. Keyboard users tab through ghost-invisible navigation; screen-reader-only users have no visual anchor. Native `<button>`/`<a>` default outlines are not customized away explicitly, but the dark theme makes the default browser outline (typically blue) clash with the gold accent system. Recommended fix: add a single global rule `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }` and a thin underline variant for inline `.b-link` elements. Test tab order through dashboard, fitting tool, and admin pages — those have the most interactive density.
title: No :focus-visible styles on any interactive element
